_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize statewide-exec batch: ND Lt Gov Strinden, NE Lt Gov Kelly, NE AG Hilgers, NH AG Formella, NJ Lt Gov Caldwell. State tier. Every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence; never inferred from party.

profile.background: 40th Lt. Governor of North Dakota, elected on the Armstrong-Strinden ticket and inaugurated Dec 15, 2024. Prior: ND House of Representatives, District 41 (Fargo), 2018-2024 (Education, Appropriations; chaired interim Juvenile Justice Committee). Scored on her ND House roll-call record.

michelle-strinden.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 from primary-source ND House Journal roll calls. Reliably conservative on marquee culture-war votes: YEA on near-total abortion ban (SB 2150), all three major transgender bills (minors' medical care HB 1254, sports HB 1249, bathrooms HB 1473), ESA school choice (HB 1532), library explicit-material restriction (HB 1205), and pro-gun bills (HB 1339 self-defense/carry, HB 1340 firearms preemption). One cross-party deviation: voted NAY on SB 2360 (the CRIMINAL-penalty library obscenity bill) and to sustain Gov. Burgum's veto — but she backed the signed companion HB 1205, so this is an objection to criminalization/cost, not a pro-pornography vote (not scored as contrary). No evidence located on religious liberty, elections, immigration, or border at the individual-vote level (honest nulls).

0.note: Voted YEA on SB 2150 House passage (76-14, Apr 17 2023), a near-total abortion ban treating abortion as a Class C felony with narrow life/health + early rape-incest exceptions.

1.note: SB 2150 is a near-total ban (abolition-leaning, not merely a restriction); she voted YEA on final House passage.

2.note: YEA on HB 1254 (bans gender-transition procedures for minors, 66-25), HB 1249 (school sports by birth sex, 78-15), and HB 1473 (sex-segregated facilities, 78-14) — affirms biological sex / rejects transgender ideology.

4.note: YEA on transgender school-sports (HB 1249) and facilities (HB 1473) bills — opposes promotion of LGBTQ identity in schools/public policy.

0.note: YEA on HB 1532 final passage (54-40, Feb 21 2023), an ESA-style educational reimbursement/school-choice program; self-described school-choice advocate.

3.note: YEA on HB 1205 (signed version) prohibiting public libraries from maintaining explicit sexual material — protects minors from sexualized content.

0.note: YEA on HB 1339 (self-defense / expanded firearm carry, 93-0) and HB 1340 (firearms preemption barring local gun ordinances, 83-10) — pro-carry / opposes local gun restriction.

profile.background: 42nd Lt. Governor of Nebraska, elected as Gov. Jim Pillen's running mate (won Nov 2022, sworn Jan 5 2023); running again on the Pillen ticket in 2026. 35-year career prosecutor: elected Lancaster County Attorney, then U.S. Attorney for the District of Nebraska (2018-2021), then Criminal Bureau Chief at the NE AG's office. No legislative voting record; scored on official actions and public statements.

joe-kelly.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. As Lt. Gov he presides over the Unicameral (no policy votes). Strongest HARD evidence is a career tough-on-crime prosecutorial record (firearms, drug, trafficking, illegal-reentry enforcement; Project Guardian/Safe Neighborhoods) plus a documented public statement against NE's 2024 abortion-rights ballot amendment, and participation in Pillen's National Guard border deployment. SCORING CAUTION applied: his firearms record is ENFORCEMENT against illegal possession ('nothing to do with people who lawfully own guns'), not a 2A-rights stance — self_defense cells left null. No evidence found on marriage/sexuality, school choice, religious liberty, economy/CBDC, elections, or federalism (honest nulls); no issue-group ratings located.

0.note: At a Business and Professional People for Life luncheon (Sep 18 2024) publicly argued against NE Initiative 439 (Protect the Right to Abortion), warning it would bar any regulation — a pro-life public stance.

Career prosecutor and U.S. Attorney who launched Project Guardian / Project Safe Neighborhoods and made enforcing firearms/violent-crime laws 'a top priority' — affirmative backing of law enforcement.

2.note: As U.S. Attorney and NE Criminal Bureau Chief prosecuted traffickers, child sexual exploitation, and violent crime to sentence ('no higher priority than the prosecution of people who commit these evil acts') — proportional punishment of evildoers.

0.note: Addressed NE Army National Guard soldiers on behalf of the administration before their 2023 deployment supporting Texas at the southern border — supports active military border presence.

profile.background: 33rd Attorney General of Nebraska (since Jan 5 2023); formally launched 2026 reelection (unopposed in the May 2026 GOP primary). Prior: NE state senator (District 21, 2017-2023) and Speaker of the Legislature (2021-2023). Scored on his AG litigation/coalition record plus documented legislative leadership.

mike-hilgers.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06 from a deep, uniformly conservative HARD record. As Speaker he made the LB 933 abortion trigger ban his 2022 priority bill. As AG he has led or joined dozens of cited actions: pro-life mifepristone briefs (led 21- and 23-state coalitions), anti-trans-mandate litigation (Title IX, Skrmetti, sports, bathrooms), child-online-safety/parental-rights bills and briefs, religious-liberty briefs, an extensive anti-ESG campaign (Net Zero, Vanguard coal deal, sued ISS), election-integrity actions (citizenship verification), tough-on-crime prosecutions, aggressive anti-federal-mandate litigation (ATF, EPA WOTUS, student loans), pro-Texas-border-barrier and anti-sanctuary briefs, and pro-2A ATF-rule challenges + concealed-carry reciprocity. No cross-party deviations found. Genuine nulls: CBDC, balanced-budget, individual 2017-22 roll calls (DB did not render), and numeric issue-group ratings.

0.note: As Speaker designated the LB 933 abortion trigger-ban as his personal priority bill (2022) and pledged a special session to restrict abortion if Roe fell — affirms protection of unborn life.

1.note: LB 933 trigger ban (priority bill) sought to outlaw abortion; as AG led/joined 21- and 23-state coalitions defending abortion-drug restrictions — active abolition-leaning advocacy.

2.note: Praised Skrmetti (upholding TN ban on minors' gender transition), stopped Biden Title IX gender-identity rule, joined briefs against gender ideology — affirms biological sex.

4.note: Briefs to protect Title IX women's sports and against a school policy allowing boys in girls' bathrooms — opposes LGBTQ-identity promotion in public schools/policy.

1.note: Co-introduced the Parental Rights in Social Media Act (age verification + parental consent) and joined a 21-state parental-rights coalition — parental consent on minors' interventions.

2.note: Joined brief supporting parents' right to opt elementary children out of gender/sex-ed instruction — opposes gender-ideology curricula.

3.note: Co-introduced legislation criminalizing AI-generated CSAM and requiring age verification/parental consent for minors online — protects minors from sexualized content.

0.note: Joined brief supporting religious liberty and free-speech rights of churches — affirms right to publicly profess faith.

1.note: Pushed back on Biden rescinding student religious-group protections; brief supporting religious liberty for private preschools — conscience/religious exemptions.

4.note: Led/joined anti-ESG actions: Net Zero alliance letter, antitrust suit over ESG commitments, Vanguard coal agreement, sued proxy-advisor ISS over ESG — opposes ESG capture, anti-trust action.

1.note: Joined suit supporting an EO requiring citizenship verification in federal elections and challenged Biden voter-registration EO 14019 — supports citizenship-verified voter rolls.

0.note: Joined brief protecting privacy of public servants (police/officials) to reduce violence risk; pro-enforcement AG record — backs law enforcement.

2.note: AG office secured numerous life/long sentences for murder and child sexual assault (e.g. quadruple-murder conviction 2025) — proportional punishment of evildoers.

1.note: Sued to block Biden student-loan forgiveness and led comment opposing DOE appliance/stove mandates — resists unconstitutional federal mandates.

2.note: Litigated against federal overreach on guns (ATF rules), life (FDA mifepristone), and education (Title IX) — resists federal overreach across issues.

3.note: Joined multistate suit against EPA WOTUS rule expressly 'to protect states' sovereignty' over land/water — affirms state sovereignty against unconstitutional federal action.

0.note: Joined 27-state coalition supporting Texas border-defense barriers — supports physical border barrier/security.

2.note: Joined brief supporting federal immigration enforcement against Minnesota sanctuary posture — opposes sanctuary policies / supports federal preemption.

0.note: Urged U.S. House to pass concealed-carry reciprocity; his 2017 firearms-preemption language fed NE's 2023 constitutional-carry law (LB 77) — supports permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: Sued over the ATF 'engaged in the business' rule (a registry/background-expansion rule) — opposes federal gun-registry-style restrictions.

4.note: Multiple suits against ATF pistol-brace, engaged-in-business, and self-assembly-kit rules — opposes ATF overreach.

profile.background: 31st Attorney General of New Hampshire (since 2021). NH AG is APPOINTED by the Governor and confirmed by the Executive Council, NOT elected — originally appointed by Gov. Sununu, unanimously reconfirmed to a second term under Gov. Ayotte on Sep 17 2025; he is not a 2026 ballot candidate. Served as 2025 NAAG president. Scored on his AG litigation/coalition record.

john-formella.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CANDIDACY CORRECTION: prior record tagged him 'incumbent_seeking_reelection' — wrong; NH AG is appointed, and he was reconfirmed by the Executive Council Sep 17 2025 (no 2026 election). Conservative LITIGATION record on guns (Mass./ATF suits), trans sports (Aug-2024 amicus, defends NH's HB 1205), school choice (defended the Education Freedom Account program), religious liberty (26-state brief), anti-ESG letters, EPA/ATF federalism suits, and northern-border/DACA immigration action. DOCUMENTED CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION/ABSTENTION ON ABORTION: declined the Apr-2024 multistate mifepristone brief that every other New England AG joined, but also filed NO affirmative pro-life litigation — so all sanctity_of_life cells are honest nulls, not contrary. Generally bipartisan/institutional posture. Genuine nulls: CBDC, same-sex marriage, abortion, parental-rights suit, porn age-verification (SB 648 still pending).

2.note: Joined 25 GOP AGs in Aug-2024 amicus urging SCOTUS to uphold transgender-sports bans (Title IX = biological sex), and defends NH's own trans-sports law HB 1205 — affirms biological sex.

0.note: Successfully defended NH's Education Freedom Account (school-choice voucher) program against a union constitutional challenge; the suit was dismissed — supports school choice.
Joined a 26-state amicus supporting religious liberty (challenging a transit authority's ban on religious advertising), calling the policy unconstitutional — affirms public religious expression.

4.note: Signed multistate letters pressing BlackRock over ESG/Climate Action 100+ and accusing the Net Zero Financial Services alliance of antitrust/consumer violations — opposes ESG capture.

1.note: Defended NH's GOP voter-ID / absentee-ballot ID law (HB 1569), stating the legislature acted to 'strengthen the integrity' of elections — supports photo voter ID.

0.note: Made fentanyl/drug-trafficking enforcement his signature issue (2025 NAAG presidential initiative), strengthened the Drug Prosecution Unit, led coalitions for the HALT Fentanyl Act — affirmative backing of law enforcement.

1.note: Joined 25-state coalition seeking an emergency stay of the EPA's 2024 power-plant rule on cost/reliability grounds — resists unconstitutional federal mandates.

2.note: Joined multistate suits against ATF private-sale and pistol-brace rules, calling them 'illegal overreach by unelected federal officials' — resists federal overreach on guns.

3.note: Joined multistate EPA challenges citing state energy cost/reliability — affirms state authority against federal action.

0.note: Co-launched (with Gov. Sununu) the Northern Border Alliance Task Force (~$1.4M, ~10,000 added patrol hours near the Canadian border) — supports active border-enforcement presence.

1.note: Joined multistate suit against the ATF private-firearm-sale rule (a background/registry-expansion rule) — opposes federal gun restrictions.

4.note: Sued over ATF pistol-brace rule and filed a 24-state SCOTUS amicus challenging Mass. gun laws as applied to NH residents — opposes ATF/citizen-disarmament overreach.

profile.religion: Christian — ordained United Methodist minister (pastor, Covenant UMC, Plainfield NJ)
profile.background: 4th Lt. Governor of New Jersey and 35th Secretary of State, sworn in Jan 20 2026 after the Sherrill/Caldwell Democratic ticket won the Nov 4 2025 gubernatorial election. Ordained United Methodist minister; longtime charter-school administrator (NJ Charter School Administrator of the Year, 2015) and 26-year member of the New Brunswick Board of Education. (Prior record had no 'mayor of Fanwood' — that premise was unsupported and is corrected.)

dale-caldwell.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06. CANDIDACY CONFIRMED: Sherrill/Caldwell won Nov 4 2025 (~57%); Caldwell sworn in as LG + Secretary of State Jan 20 2026. Progressive-aligned Democrat: ran on a pro-choice platform (Planned Parenthood Action Fund endorsed the ticket), serves in an administration that codified statewide sanctuary policy (Immigrant Trust Act, Mar 2026), and the ticket was endorsed by gun-control groups (Everytown, Moms Demand, Giffords, Brady). NOTED CONSERVATIVE-ADJACENT CROSS-CURRENT: he is a documented charter-school founder/administrator and the ticket supports expanding charters / public-school choice — but this is PUBLIC charter/inter-district choice, NOT vouchers/ESAs (the ticket opposed vouchers and was NJEA-endorsed), so it does not meet the universal-school-choice rubric bar and is left null with this note rather than scored true. FALSE cells below are administration/ticket-level positions (he is the elected #2 and chief elections officer), each cited; no personal Caldwell statement on marriage, crime, taxes, or religious-liberty policy was found (honest nulls). SOURCING CAUTION: any pro-life/pro-marriage 'Dale G. Caldwell' positions on iVoterGuide belong to a different Colorado Republican, not this man.

0.note: Ran as LG on the Sherrill/Caldwell ticket whose platform is to codify abortion rights and expand reproductive/IVF access — does not affirm life-at-conception. Administration/ticket-level position.

4.note: Planned Parenthood Action Fund of NJ endorsed the Sherrill/Caldwell ticket (Jun 2025) — the ticket accepted/benefited from abortion-industry backing. Ticket-level.

2.note: The Sherrill/Caldwell administration he serves as LG signed the NJ Immigrant Trust Act (Mar 2026) codifying statewide sanctuary policy and limiting ICE cooperation — supports sanctuary policy. Administration-level.

0.note: Ran on a gun-control ticket endorsed by Everytown, Moms Demand, Giffords PAC, and Brady PAC; record of supporting assault-weapon restrictions and universal background checks — opposes permitless/constitutional carry. Ticket-level.

1.note: Gun-control-group-endorsed ticket supporting assault-weapon restrictions, magazine limits, and universal background checks — does not oppose those measures. Ticket-level.
